rulings.Case Title: SANDRA PEDROIA VS. SPECTRUM JUNGLE LAB, CORP. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 24, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT SPECTRUM BRANDS, INC. ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings The Court grants judgment on the pleadings without leave to amend. CCP 335.1 [2 year statute of limitations] bars plaintiff's action. Under 11 USC 362(c)(2), the bankruptcy stay was lifted when the bankruptcy court issued the reorganization/discharge order on July 15, 2009. Pursuant to 11 USC 108(c), plaintiff had until " 30 days after notice of the termination or expiration of the stay" to file her action. Plaintiff's declaration in opposition to the motion for summary judgment admits that she had notice that the stay was lifted by December 1 or 4 of 2009. The Court can take judicial notice of that affidavit. See Del E. Webb Corp. v. Structural Materials Co. (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d 593, 604-605 ("The court will take judicial notice of records such as admissions, answers to interrogatories, affidavits, and the like, when considering a demurrer, only where they contain statements of the plaintiff or his agent which are inconsistent with the allegations of the pleading before the court.") Plaintiff filed this untimely action more than 30 days later on January 29, 2010. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: EVELYN LEE VS. UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 24, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT ALYCE LEE, THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Motion For Summary Judgment The Court denies the motion without prejudice. On January 14, 2014, the Court gave plaintiff leave to file a third amended complaint. That filing, which is due any day, moots the instant motion. See State Compensation Insurance Fund v. Sup. Crt. (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1130-1131. Defendant may renew this motion or file a new motion for summary judgment/adjudication directed to the operative pleading. The Court further notes that plaintiff's opposition to the instant motion exceeds the page limitation requirements of CRC 3.1113(d) by 17 pages and plaintiff did not obtain leave of court to file an oversized brief. rulings.Case Title: NICOLE WOOLDRIDGE VS. ARTHUR STEVEN BROWN, ALSO KNOWN AS STEVE BROWN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Norman Sabel'S Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 24, 2014, Line 4 - Defendant Norman Sabel'S Motion For Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion is DENIED. The motion is based on Pacers, pursuant to which a court may, under certain circumstances, stay civil discovery pending a parallel criminal proceeding. Pacers, however, only applies where ¿both civil and criminal proceedings arise out of the same or related transactions.¿ Pacers, Inc. v. Superior Court, 162 Cal. App. 3d 686, 690 (1984). Here, the incident out of which the criminal action arose happened many months after this lawsuit was filed and, thus, is based on different facts. As Mr. Sabel admits, the criminal charges ¿do not presently directly involve Ms. Woolridge or her claims.¿ Accordingly, the criminal matter and the instant matter do not ¿arise out of the same or related transactions,¿ and Pacers, thus, does not support Mr. Sabel¿s position. In the event that Mr. Sabel believes that any particular question at his deposition implicates his rights under the Fifth Amendment, it is incumbent upon him to invoke his rights as to that particular question. See Warford v. Medeiros, 160 Cal. App. 3d 1035, 1045 (1984) (Fifth Amendment must be ¿specifically claimed on a particular question¿). Insofar as this motion involves a request to avoid testifying in ¿blanket¿ fashion, it must be rejected. Id. Because Defendant Sabel did not have a substantial justification for seeking to avoid his deposition, sanctions shall be awarded to Plaintiff in the amount of $4,912.50. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to TSchoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NICOLE WOOLDRIDGE VS. ARTHUR STEVEN BROWN, ALSO KNOWN AS STEVE BROWN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion For An Order Compelling Defendant Norman Sabel'S Deposition And Awarding Plaintiff Monetary Sanctions.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 24, 2014, Line 4 - Plaintiff'S Motion For An Order Compelling Defendant Norman Sabel'S Deposition And Awarding Plaintiff Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion is GRANTED. Defendant Sabel¿s rationale for avoiding his deposition is based on Pacers, pursuant to which a court may, under certain circumstances, stay civil discovery pending a parallel criminal proceeding. Pacers, however, only applies where ¿both civil and criminal proceedings arise out of the same or related transactions.¿ Pacers, Inc. v. Superior Court, 162 Cal. App. 3d 686, 690 (1984). Here, the incident out of which the criminal action arose happened many months after this lawsuit was filed and, thus, is based on different facts. As Mr. Sabel admits, the criminal charges ¿do not presently directly involve Ms. Woolridge or her claims.¿ Accordingly, the criminal matter and the instant matter do not ¿arise out of the same or related transactions,¿ and Pacers, thus, does not support Mr. Sabel¿s position. In the event that Mr. Sabel believes that any particular question at his deposition implicates his rights under the Fifth Amendment, it is incumbent upon him to invoke his rights as to that particular question. See Warford v. Medeiros, 160 Cal. App. 3d 1035, 1045 (1984) (Fifth Amendment must be ¿specifically claimed on a particular question¿). Insofar as Mr. Sabel¿s position involves a request to avoid testifying in ¿blanket¿ fashion, it must be rejected. Id. In addition, Plaintiff¿s request for judicial notice is GRANTED. Because Defendant Sabel did not have a substantial justification for seeking to avoid his deposition, sanctions are appropriately awarded to Plaintiff. However, because the issues in this motion are largely duplicative of the issues that were already put before the Court in Defendant Sabel¿s motion for protective order, the amount of sanctions awarded to Plaintiff in connection with this motion shall be limited to $325. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to TSchoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NICOLE WOOLDRIDGE VS. ARTHUR STEVEN BROWN, ALSO KNOWN AS STEVE BROWN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion For Further Discovery Responses By Defendants To Plaintiff'S Requests For Production Of Documents And Things (Set One); Special Interrogatories (Set Two) And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 24, 2014, Line 4 - Plaintiff'S Motion For Further Discovery Responses By Defendants To Plaintiff'S Requests For Production Of Documents And Things (Set One); Special Interrogatories (Set Two) And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion GRANTED as to requests for production 1, 3, 4, 5, 8, 9, 12, 13, 15, 16, 17, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27, 28, 29, 30, 33, 34, 35, 36 and 37. Defendant Sabel¿s responses to the foregoing requests do not comply with CCP section 2031.210, which requires a responding party to provide a verified response containing a statement that the party will comply, a representation of inability to comply, or an objection. Motion DENIED as to requests for production 6 and 15 insofar as Defendant Sabel responded to these requests with a representation that he lacks the ability to comply with the requests. However, the motion is GRANTED as to requests for production 6 and 15 insofar as Defendant Sabel has not verified his responses to these requests. Motion GRANTED as to special interrogatories 25, 26 and 35. Mr. Sabel has not provided a substantive response to said special interrogatories, and his objections do not justify his failure to provide a substantive response. Because Defendant Sabel¿s position is not substantially justified, sanctions are awarded to Plaintiff in the amount of $1,875. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to TSchoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT CARTER et al VS. PACIFIC UNION REAL ESTATE GROUP LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Brookfield Asset Management Inc And Brps Llc'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 24, 2014, Line 18, Defendants Brookfield Asset Management Inc And Brps Llc'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Summary Adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted as to exhibits 1-7, to the extent that the motions were filed, and exhibits 8-21 to the extent that the articles were published. Plaintiffs' request for judicial notice is granted. As to the first cause of action, summary adjudication is denied. The Court can amend a judgment to add a non-party alter ego. (See, e.g. Leek v. Cooper 194 Cal. App. 4th 399, 419.) Further, there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether a unity of interest exists between Defendants and PUREG and whether an inequitable result will follow. (See Evidence Cited in Support of Plaintiffs' Separate Statement of Facts in Support of Opposition ("PSS") 45-60; see also Evidence Cited in Support of Plaintiffs' Separate Statement of Additional Material Facts ("AMF") 18-28.) As to the fourth cause of action, summary adjudication is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Defendants' ever loaned PUREG money. (See Evidence Cited in Support of PSS 62-63.) As to the fifth cause of action, summary adjudication is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the alleged transfers were fraudulent under Civ. Code § 3432. (See Evidence Cited in Support of PSS 62-63.) As to the third cause of action, summary adjudication is granted. Defendants shift the burden by producing prima facie evidence that the insurance policy did not exist. (See Evidence Cited in Support of Defendant's Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 62.) Plaintiffs do not address the insurance policy in their Points and Authorities and do not produce any admissible evidence to controvert. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: ASHWIN NANDA VS. WESTERN DENTAL SERVICES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 24, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT ABDULLAH MUNSAF, ANDRE AZARINFAR, WESTERN DENTAL SERVICES, INC. Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings Granted, without leave to amend. A dismissal with prejudice is a final adjudication on the merits for the purposes of res judicata. (Boeken v. Philip Morris USA, Inc. (2010) 48 Cal.4th 788, 793.) While it is regrettable that Plaintiff inadvertently foreclosed his ability to proceed in state court on this primary right, he has done so. (See Samuels v. Northern Telecom, Inc. (1991) 942 F.2d 834.) This Court cannot offer the redress Plaintiff seeks unless the dismissal in federal court is set aside or reconsidered. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BUTHINA RASHID, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF ALL VS. EINSTEIN NOAH RESTAURANT GROUP, INC., A DELAWARE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Litigation
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 24, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANT EINSTEIN NOAH RESTAURANT GROUP, INC., A DELAWARE Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Litigation Denied. Although there is a divergence between Federal and California case law on the issues raised in this motion, and these issues are currently being reviewed by the California Supreme Court (Iskanian v. CLS Transportation of Los Angeles, California Supreme Court Case No. S204032), the Court must follow current California law. The agreement at issue here provides in pertinent part: "Any claim purporting to be brought as a class action, collective action, or representative action will be decided as an individual claim." (Declaration of Mark A. Neubauer, Ex. 1.) A PAGA claim is a representative action, and therefore PAGA claims are waived here, pursuant to the terms of the agreement. (Brown v. Ralphs Grocery Co. (2011) 197 Cal.App.4th 489, 499.) PAGA waivers are unenforceable as against public policy, and this finding of unenforceability is not preempted by the Federal Arbitration Act ("FAA"). (Id. at 503.) An agreement that contains both a PAGA waiver and a class action waiver, such as the agreement in this case, is "as a whole tainted with illegality and is unenforceable." (Franco v. Athens Disposal Co., Inc. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1277, 1303.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg
